source_pr.title: executor: fix file exists errors in tests for `select into outfile`
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #19718, close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/19337 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: executor: fix file exists errors in tests for `select into outfile`

### What is changed and how it works?
Use random file names to avoid file name conflict.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- executor: fix file exists errors in tests for `select into outfile`

symptoms: Tests for `SELECT ... INTO OUTFILE` intermittently fail with "file exists" errors due to outfile name/path collisions across runs.
failure_signature: `select into outfile` tests fail with "file exists" when multiple runs share the same temp outfile name/path
root_cause_explanation: The tests wrote outfiles to `os.TempDir()` using fixed or partially-fixed filenames (e.g. `select-into-outfile.data`). When tests run concurrently or when leftover files exist from another run, the outfile path can collide and cause intermittent "file exists" failures. This PR stabilizes the tests by generating a unique per-test outfile path (`randomSelectFilePath`) so each test writes to an isolated filename.
fix_pattern: Avoid hard-coded/shared outfile names in tests; generate a unique per-test temp outfile path to prevent filesystem collisions.

evidence: evidence: PR title: executor: fix file exists errors in tests for `select into outfile`
evidence: use per-test temp outfile path via `randomSelectFilePath` to avoid file-collision failures (executor/select_into_test.go)
